title: Spiced Tofu Veggie Wrap
description: Portable high-protein wrap with masala tofu, crunchy vegetables, and a tangy sauce. Desk- and travel-friendly.
explanation: This wrap is designed for lunch boxes and travel days when you need something filling that holds up without a full kitchen. The tofu filling can be cooked ahead; assemble just before eating if you want maximum crunch.
Use any flatbread you have—roti, tortilla, or lavash. A dry filling travels better than a saucy one.
ingredients: 1/2 tsp Chaat Masala or Garam Masala
instructions: Saute tofu in oil with turmeric, masala, chilli, and salt until lightly dry and seasoned (5–6 minutes).
Warm the roti or tortilla so it flexes without cracking.
Spread chutney, add tofu, then cucumber, carrot, and greens.
Squeeze lemon, roll tightly, and wrap in paper or foil if packing.
stepNotes: Press tofu briefly if watery so the wrap does not sog.
Cook filling drier for travel; keep it softer for home lunch.
Put sauce in the center, not near edges, to reduce leaks.
If packing more than 2 hours ahead, keep wet veggies separate.
A second wrap freezes poorly—make fresh or refrigerate 1 day max.

variationSections.title: Filling swaps
items: Use leftover chana, rajma, or soy chunks instead of tofu.
Hummus or peanut butter instead of chutney for a different flavor.
Add roasted peppers or leftover sabzi.

variationSections.title: Travel tips
items: Wrap in foil and keep upright in a lunch bag.
Skip tomato if packing for long hours—it releases water.
Carry lemon and salt sachets to refresh before eating.
